text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST
Restaging examination

CLINICAL INDICATION: 62-year-old female with stage IIIB (T3N2M0) right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant. Currently on osimertinib (TAGRISSO) 80 mg daily as first-line targeted therapy, now approximately 9 months into treatment. Prior restaging CT at 3 months demonstrated marked reduction of the right lower lobe primary and near-resolution of mediastinal adenopathy. This examination is requested for interval response assessment.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis acquired in the portal venous phase following intravenous administration of 100 mL of iohexol 350 contrast material. Coronal and sagittal reformations generated. Comparison made to prior CT dated approximately 3 months ago (first restaging scan) and to the pre-treatment baseline CT obtained at initial staging.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The trachea and central bronchi are patent. There is a residual mass-like consolidation in the right lower lobe, posterior segment, measuring approximately 1.8 cm in greatest axial dimension. This represents substantial interval decrease compared to 2.9 cm on the 3-month restaging scan and a marked reduction from the 4.8 cm pre-treatment baseline. The lesion maintains a slightly irregular, ill-defined margin posterolaterally but overall demonstrates continued involution with loss of its previously prominent spiculated character. No new satellite nodules or additional pulmonary masses identified. No new ground-glass opacities or interstitial changes to suggest drug-related pneumonitis. The left lung is clear. No consolidation, pleural-based lesions, or new nodules identified bilaterally.

Mediastinum and Lymph Nodes:
No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes are identified on this examination. The right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph nodes, previously measuring up to approximately 1.5 cm at baseline and having decreased to sub-centimeter on the 3-month scan, are now below the threshold of confident detection — a cluster of very small nodes measuring 4-5 mm is present in the right paratracheal region but these are felt to represent residual normal-sized nodes. The previously enlarged subcarinal (station 7) nodes are similarly no longer discretely identifiable as enlarged structures. No contralateral mediastinal or supraclavicular adenopathy.

Pleura and Pericardium:
No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No pericardial effusion. No pleural thickening or masses.

Chest Wall and Bony Thorax:
No osseous lesions suspicious for metastatic involvement. No rib fractures. No soft tissue masses of the chest wall. Visualized spine and posterior elements appear intact.

Cardiovascular:
Heart is normal in size. The aorta and great vessels are normal in caliber without aneurysmal change. No pulmonary arterial filling defects to suggest pulmonary embolism on this non-dedicated study.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with homogeneous parenchymal enhancement. No focal hepatic lesions. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein patent.

Gallbladder and Biliary Tract:
Gallbladder is present, no stones or wall thickening. Common bile duct is within normal limits.

Spleen:
Spleen is normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas:
The pancreas enhances uniformly with no focal masses or ductal dilatation.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands appear normal in morphology and attenuation. No discrete nodules or masses identified in either adrenal gland. No adrenal enlargement.

Kidneys and Ureters:
Bilateral kidneys are normal in size with symmetric cortical enhancement. No masses, cysts, or hydronephrosis. No ureteral dilation.

Gastrointestinal:
No bowel obstruction. No free intraperitoneal air. No abnormal bowel wall thickening. No mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.

Retroperitoneum:
Aorta and IVC normal in caliber. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.

Ascites:
None.

---

PELVIS:

No pelvic lymphadenopathy. Uterus and adnexa are within normal limits for age. Bladder wall smooth, no filling defects. No free fluid.

Osseous structures of the pelvis and proximal femora: No lytic or sclerotic lesions to suggest osseous metastatic disease.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Continued marked shrinkage of the right lower lobe primary lesion, now measuring approximately 1.8 cm, down from 2.9 cm at the 3-month restaging scan and 4.8 cm at pre-treatment baseline. The lesion has diminished in both size and conspicuity, with loss of much of its prior spiculated architecture.

2. Previously noted mediastinal lymphadenopathy at the right paratracheal (4R) and subcarinal (7) stations is no longer appreciable. Only a few normal-sized residual nodes in the right paratracheal region are seen.

3. No new pulmonary lesions, no hepatic lesions, no adrenal nodules, and no bony lesions identified. No new sites of thoracic or extrathoracic disease.

4. No imaging findings to suggest pulmonary drug toxicity (e.g., osimertinib-associated pneumonitis).

Overall, imaging is consistent with an ongoing and deepening response to osimertinib therapy. The disease burden in both the chest primary and mediastinal nodal stations has improved substantially compared to prior studies.
